message:
test(create): assert parent-standin resolves to the actual epic ID with parent-child dep type

Continuation of the assertion-hardening pass in the create tests.
`test_create_parent_standin_resolution` previously called
`br create -f <markdown> --assert success` and stopped there — it
did not verify the parent-standin references in the markdown
actually resolved to the in-session epic, nor that the linkage used
the `parent-child` dependency type.

Rewrite:
- Capture stdout of `br create -f <markdown> --json` via
  `Command::output()` and assert exit success with stderr in the
  panic message.
- Parse the JSON; assert exactly two imported issues (the epic +
  the task that references it as its parent).
- Find each imported issue by title. Assert the epic has
  `issue_type == "epic"` and capture its ID.
- Assert the task's `dependencies` array contains at least one
  entry with `depends_on_id == <epic_id>` AND
  `type == "parent-child"`. Failure message includes both the
  expected epic ID and the actual dependencies array so diagnostic
  output is adequate without a separate print.

Imports `serde_json::Value` at the top of the file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
